data.title: ProPetro targets 150-200 MW capacity with PROPWR expansion by 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ProPetro Holding Corp. (NYSE:PUMP) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Sam Sledge highlighted the company's transition towards next-generation service offerings, emphasizing that 75% of their fleet now consists of Tier IV dual-fuel and electric equipment, which remains in high demand. He also announced plans to deploy a fifth FORCE electric fleet in 2025 under long-term contracts to stabilize earnings.

  • The PROPWR business was presented as a transformational growth opportunity for ProPetro. Sledge revealed an initial order of over 140 megawatts of natural gas-powered generation equipment, with deliveries expected in the second half of 2025 and early 2026. The company is actively negotiating long-term contracts for these assets.

  • CFO David Schorlemer noted a 26% sequential decline in adjusted EBITDA to $53 million for Q4 2024, impacted by seasonality and market softness. Despite this, he pointed out that free cash flow generation remained solid, supported by reduced capital expenditures.

  • Schorlemer also emphasized the company's focus on maintaining a strong liquidity position, with $161 million in total liquidity as of year-end 2024, and highlighted reduced capital intensity due to the transition to electric fleets.

Outlook

  • ProPetro anticipates deploying a fifth FORCE electric fleet mid-2025 and sees significant growth potential in the PROPWR business, targeting 150-200 megawatts of power generation capacity by early 2026.

  • The company expects full-year 2025 capital expenditures to range between $300 million and $400 million, with $150 million to $200 million allocated to PROPWR growth. Schorlemer confirmed that $104 million in financing has already been secured for PROPWR investments.

  • Sledge expressed optimism about North American onshore oilfield services in the coming years, supported by demand for efficiency and cost-saving solutions.

Financial Results

  • Fourth-quarter 2024 revenue was $321 million, an 11% sequential decline attributed to seasonality and extended customer holiday shutdowns.

  • The company reported a net loss of $17 million for Q4, including a $24 million non-cash impairment related to wireline goodwill.

  • Capital expenditures for Q4 were $25 million, with significant reductions from prior quarters. ProPetro emphasized the benefits of transitioning to electric fleets, which reduced maintenance costs and capital intensity.

  • Liquidity remained strong, with $50 million in cash and $111 million in available credit capacity under the ABL Credit Facility.

Q&A

  • Kurt Hallead, Benchmark: Asked about the potential revenue and EBITDA contributions from PROPWR’s 150-200 megawatts of power generation capacity. CFO Schorlemer estimated $300,000 to $400,000 EBITDA per megawatt annually, with returns in the high teens.

  • Eddie Kim, Barclays: Questioned the outlook for 2025 frac fleet utilization. Sledge confirmed expectations to maintain 14-15 active fleets, highlighting strong performance from high-quality customers and ongoing attrition in lower-end competitors.

  • Waqar Syed, ATB Capital Markets: Requested specific guidance for Q1 2025. Schorlemer responded that activity levels are trending upward and expected Q1 to perform similarly to Q2 and Q3 2024.

  • Donald Crist, Johnson Rice: Inquired about operational risks in the PROPWR business. Sledge emphasized leveraging ProPetro’s infrastructure and expertise, alongside building a specialized team, to mitigate risks.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about seasonality, pricing pressures, and risks associated with new business ventures like PROPWR. The tone was slightly skeptical but balanced with interest in long-term growth opportunities.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic growth and operational resilience. However, responses during the Q&A revealed a cautious approach to guidance and potential market challenges.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed greater enthusiasm for growth initiatives like PROPWR, while acknowledging near-term headwinds in pricing and utilization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to focus on long-term growth opportunities in PROPWR, compared to Q3’s emphasis on maintaining fleet utilization and capital discipline.

  • Management tone was more optimistic regarding strategic initiatives, highlighting progress in electrification and diversification, while analysts maintained a cautious stance on market conditions.

  • Financial results reflected a sequential decline in revenue and adjusted EBITDA, primarily due to seasonality, contrasting with Q3’s slight revenue growth and improved EBITDA.

Risks and Concerns

  • Seasonality and market softness impacted Q4 results, with extended customer holiday shutdowns reducing utilization.

  • Pricing pressures in conventional diesel-only equipment and wireline services remain a challenge.

  • Analysts highlighted operational risks in scaling the PROPWR business and the need for long-term contracts to ensure stability.

Final Takeaway

ProPetro Holding Corp. is aggressively transitioning towards next-generation offerings, with 75% of its fleet already using Tier IV dual-fuel and electric equipment. The PROPWR business is positioned as a key growth driver, targeting 150-200 megawatts of capacity by 2026. Despite seasonal headwinds and pricing pressures, the company maintains a strong liquidity position and continues to execute its capital allocation strategy focused on fleet electrification, growth investments, and shareholder returns. Investors should monitor the success of PROPWR contracts and electric fleet deployments as potential catalysts for future financial performance.

data.title: KKR weighs £4B equity investment in Thames Water - Bloomberg
data.description:
Asset manager KKR (NYSE:KKR) has offered nearly £4B (~$5B) for a majority stake in struggling U.K. utility Thames Water in a management-led buyout, Bloomberg reported earlier Wednesday.

KKR (NYSE:KKR), which has been expanding its investments in infrastructure, does not plan to sell any assets of Thames Water or to make structural changes if it gains control of the business, according to the report, which also said talks are continuing with no guarantee a deal will be completed.

The U.K.'s largest water and sewage company is struggling under more than £16B of debt and without fresh capital, it could plunge into special administration, a state-supervised process similar to insolvency designed for bankrupt businesses that provide critical services.

The cash-strapped company bought itself some more time earlier this week when a London judge approved an emergency loan worth as much as £3B from existing senior creditors, which will allow Thames Water to keep operating beyond the end of March, when it has said it would run out of cash.

data.title: B2Gold Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.05, revenue of $499.79M in-line
data.description:
• B2Gold press release (NYSE:BTG): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $499.79M (-2.4% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Total gold production of 186,001 ounces in Q4 2024: Total gold production in the fourth quarter of 2024 was 186,001 ounces.

  • Outlook

    Total gold production in 2025 is anticipated to be between 970,000 and 1,075,000 ounces, a significant increase from 2024 production levels primarily due to the scheduled mining and processing of higher-grade ore from the Fekola Phase 7 and Cardinal pits made accessible by the deferred stripping campaign that was undertaken throughout 2024, the expected contribution from Fekola Regional starting in mid-2025, the commencement of mining of higher-grade ore at Fekola underground and the commencement of gold production at the Goose Project by the end of the second quarter of 2025. The Company's full year total cash operating costs for the Fekola Complex, Masbate and Otjikoto are forecast to be between $835 and $895 per gold ounce and total all-in sustaining costs are forecast to be between $1,460 and $1,520 per gold ounce. Operating cost guidance for the Goose Project will be released in the second quarter of 2025 (prior to the commencement of initial production), after the publication in the first quarter of 2025 of B2Gold's initial Goose Project life of mine plan based on updated Mineral Reserves

data.title: ProMIS Neurosciences dismisses COO position
data.description:
• ProMIS Neurosciences (NASDAQ:PMN) said on February 14, 2025, it separated with Gavin T. Malenfant as Chief Operating Officer of the company.
  • The company also terminated the Chief Operating Officer position, and does not intend to replace Malenfant.

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 3.3M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a build of 3.3M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending February 14.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a build of 2.8M barrels for the week, distillate inventories show a draw of 2.7M barrels, and stockpiles at the Cushing, Oklahoma, storage hub show a build of 1.7M barrels.

The Energy Information Administration will release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report on Thursday; analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast domestic commercial crude stocks will increase by 2.4M barrels, gasoline inventories are expected to remain unchanged, and distillate inventories will decline by 1.7M barrels.

WTI April crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $72.25/bbl after settling Wednesday at $72.10/bbl.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:USO)
